title: JSON
description: Understand JSON — the data format that powers web APIs.

blocks.content:
**JavaScript Object Notation (JSON)** is a lightweight text format used to store and exchange data between systems. It is the standard format returned by almost every web API.

JSON represents data as **key-value pairs** wrapped in curly braces `{}`. Keys are always strings, and values can be strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, objects, or `null`.

blocks.caption: A simple JSON object with three key-value pairs
Supported value types in JSON
rows: A nested set of key-value pairs
Represents an empty or missing value

blocks.content: A JSON **array** is an ordered list of values enclosed in square brackets `[]`. Each item is separated by a comma. Arrays are commonly used when an API returns multiple records — for example, a list of products or a set of search results.

blocks.caption: A JSON array containing three user objects

blocks.content: Objects can contain other objects, allowing you to represent complex, hierarchical data. A value for one key can itself be a `{}` object with its own key-value pairs.

blocks.caption: JSON with two levels of nesting

blocks.title: JSON vs JavaScript Objects
blocks.content: JSON looks like a JavaScript object, but there are key differences: JSON keys must always be in double quotes, and JSON cannot contain functions or comments.

blocks.title: JSON Quiz
blocks.description: Check your understanding of JSON structure and data types.

questions.question: Which symbol wraps a JSON object?
questions.explanation: JSON objects are always wrapped in curly braces {}.

questions.question: In JSON, keys must always be written as:
questions.explanation: Unlike JavaScript, JSON requires all keys to be double-quoted strings.

questions.question: What does the following JSON represent?
options: An array of three user objects
A nested object with three levels
questions.explanation: Square brackets [] indicate a JSON array. This one contains three objects, each with an id and name property.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid JSON value type?
questions.explanation: JSON does not support functions. Valid value types are: string, number, boolean, null, array, and object.
